burial.plot_notes: Lake View Cemetery Cleveland Cuyahoga County Ohio per FA — possibly near family from Ohio

narrative_blocks.header: Married 1872; dead at twenty-seven; no children left
narrative_blocks.body: Emma Cornelia 'Nellie' Dennison married Thomas Hooker in Syracuse on September 26, 1872, and died less than three years later on June 20, 1875 in East Syracuse at twenty-seven, leaving no children and a widower who would live forty-two more years without remarrying. She was buried at Lake View Cemetery in Cleveland, Ohio — far from her Syracuse home, possibly near her Ohio-born family — while her husband Thomas lived on in Syracuse until 1917, the last survivor of his generation of Hookers.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

She was buried at Lake View Cemetery in Cleveland, Ohio — far from her Syracuse home, possibly near her Ohio-born family — while her husband Thomas lived on in Syracuse until 1917, the last survivor of his generation of Hookers.

career.organization: Elbridge, New York and Lake Forest, Illinois

career.notes: EH: "since 1878 he has been engaged in manufacturing business at Syracuse, N.Y."

narrative_blocks.header: Widowed at thirty-three; never remarried; lived to 1917
narrative_blocks.body: Thomas Hooker married Emma Cornelia 'Nellie' Dennison on September 26, 1872 in Syracuse, and she died less than three years later — on June 20, 1875, at age twenty-seven in East Syracuse, barely twenty-eight months into a marriage that left no children. Thomas lived forty-two more years as a widower, dying in Syracuse on June 21, 1917 at seventy-five — the last of the four children of Henry Thomas and Mary Brown Cobb Hooker to die, outlasting even his brother Henry Cobb by nine years.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

burialCemetery.name: Lake View Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Notable Cleveland landmark cemetery; contains tomb of President James Garfield.
